libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Set JGroups tests to loopback/IPv4 defaults before touching JGroups classes: `jgroups.bind_addr=127.0.0.1` and `java.net.preferIPv4Stack=true`; pass the same properties to native test runtime args. Prefer in-process stacks such as `SHARED_LOOPBACK` or loopback TCP over multicast/external cluster discovery. If testing logging defaults, be aware `LogFactory` reads `jgroups.use.jdk_logger` and `jgroups.log_class` during static initialization, so set or clear them before first use.

deterministic_setup.reason: JGroups declares SLF4J as an optional dependency and LogFactory/Slf4jLogImpl use it for the normal optional logging integration path.
JGroups SslContextFactory reflectively probes org.wildfly.openssl classes to enable its optional OpenSSL provider path, which is relevant reachability coverage for SSL configuration.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8866 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.jgroups:jgroups:5.2.2.Final; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Real UDP multicast or default interface discovery can be flaky or unavailable in CI/native-image test environments.
Logging and SSL provider choices are static-initialization sensitive, so late property changes may not affect already initialized JGroups classes.
OpenSSL coverage may vary by platform/provider availability; local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: JGroups is a cluster/networking toolkit; meaningful native-image coverage commonly needs loopback-only networking defaults and optional integration dependencies that are not pulled by the normal scaffold.
